title: THE CELLAR
blurb: Halpern's bodega cellar. He never opens it. He's opening it for you. Sawed-off, full bandolier, don't waste shells on the rats — that's not what's down there.
briefing: Halpern called the morning after Lazzaro's. Said his father's padlock has been on that cellar door since '52, and he's only ever had the bent key, and the key only ever turned once. He's giving you the key. Walk in, clear the steps, walk back out. Don't open the second door at the bottom. There is a second door at the bottom.
epigraph: "Whatever you find down there, leave it."

debrief.win: Halpern locked the door behind you. Welded the hasp. Says he's not opening it again in his lifetime, which is the only honest thing he's said about that cellar in twenty-six years. You did good. Eat.
debrief.loss: Halpern is closing the bodega. Says it's time. Says his father would understand. The padlock goes back on the door, and the bent key goes into the river. The cellar wins this round.
debrief.sGrade: Halpern's nephew is taking over the bodega. The kid asked about the cellar door and Halpern said 'don't ever, even one time.' He'll listen, because of you. That's how this works.
